name: Hungarian Poppyseed Strudel
recipeIngredient: 2 Large eggs, yolks separated and whites beaten stiff
1/4 cup Raisins, soaked in warm water for 5 minutes then drained
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, beat the vanilla sugar with the egg yolks until the mixture is smooth and light-colored.
2. Beat the flour, lemon peel, butter and hot milk into the egg-and-sugar mixture.
3. Put the mixture into a saucepan. Bring it gently to a simmer and mix in the poppyseeds.
4. When the mixture returns to a simmer, turn off the heat. Let the mixture cool to room temperature.
5. When the mixture is cooled, mix in the raisins (soaked and drained) and grated apple, then fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites. If this filling is too thick to spread, add a little cold water.
6. Sprinkle melted butter or lard over the prepared sheet of strudel dough.
7. Spread the filling on one third of the dough and roll up the strudel.
8. Bake in a preheated 375-degree oven for about 40 minutes, or until the strudel is crisp and brown. Serve while still warm, cut into short lengths.
VANILLA SUGAR: Granulated sugar—or, in special cases, confectioner's sugar—flavored by being stored with a vanilla bean (sliced) in a tightly covered container for a week or more.
